title: Thelypteris indica
taxonomy: Thelypteris indica is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Thelypteris, which consists of around 400 species of ferns. The genus Thelypteris is further classified under the order Polypodiales, which includes over 10,000 species of ferns. This classification places Thelypteris indica within the larger group of vascular plants, which also includes trees, flowers, and other types of vegetation. The precise classification of Thelypteris indica continues to be refined as new research and discoveries are made.
uses: Thelypteris indica has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes, including the treatment of fever and rheumatism. Its leaves are also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties. In some regions, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and skin problems. Furthermore, Thelypteris indica is thought to have antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and confirm its effectiveness in treating various health conditions.

properties: Thelypteris indica, also known as the Indian maidenhair fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
